title: Chart Type Registry
description: Single source of truth mapping econometric methods (and portal-level chart kinds) to their canonical Plotly-JSON chart artifact, the result CSV/parquet that feeds it, and the pair of VIZ- / ECON- rule IDs that own production. This schema IS the registry: the instance (chart_type_registry.json) is what Evan's ECON-H4 handoff, Vera's VIZ-V3/V4/V8 production, and Ace's render_method_block loader all consult. Per META-CF, no inline forks of this table are permitted in any SOP; SOPs link here.

x-version.description: Semver of the registry instance (separate from this schema's x-version). Bumped per META-CF whenever methods are added, renamed, or their canonical bindings change.

x-owner.description: Single agent accountable for the registry instance. Must be 'vera'.

x-generated.description: ISO-8601 date the instance was last regenerated.

notes.description: Freeform top-level notes about the instance (e.g., what was added in this version).

methods.description: Map of method_name -> chart metadata. method_name is snake_case and matches the key used by Evan's ECON-H4 handoff table and Ace's render_method_block helper.

chart_binding.description: Binding of one method to its canonical chart artifact + owning rules.

expected_chart_type.description: Short, canonical chart-type key describing the visual form. Examples: bar, line, heatmap, dual_panel, sparkline, bar_by_lag, scatter, area_probability, line_with_ci.

canonical_filename_pattern.description: Canonical filename (basename) of the Plotly JSON artifact Vera writes under output/charts/{pair_id}/plotly/. pair_id NEVER appears in the filename — it lives in the directory path (VIZ-NM1). {episode_slug} placeholder is only permitted for entries with override_supported=true (META-ZI). Must end in .json.

required_result_file.description: Relative path (under results/{pair_id}/) of the upstream Evan-produced CSV/parquet that feeds this chart. Per ECON-H4, Evan's handoff table points into this field. {date} placeholder is allowed where Evan's dated subdirectory applies (e.g., core_models_{date}/...).

viz_rule_id.description: Vera-side rule ID that owns the rendering of this chart (e.g., VIZ-V3 for standalone Granger).

econ_rule_id.description: Evan-side rule ID that owns production of the required_result_file (e.g., ECON-E1 for granger_by_lag.csv). Use ECON-EX1 for exploratory files, ECON-C2 for core_models files, ECON-T1/ECON §Tournament for tournament files.

override_supported.description: True for charts that support the META-ZI canonical-override protocol (e.g., history_zoom_* episodes). When true, canonical_filename_pattern contains an {episode_slug} placeholder and lives under output/_comparison/; pair-specific override under output/charts/{pair_id}/.

consumer_page.description: Which portal page type is the primary consumer (story, evidence, strategy, methodology, landing, cross_pair).

notes.description: Freeform — what to call out during rendering, known pitfalls, historical bug references (e.g., 'S18-11: must NOT fall back to local_projections').
